definition: A byte pattern with wildcard bytes (??) used to locate code or data in memory after ASLR. Common in internal and external scanners.
Address Space Layout Randomization — Windows randomizes module base addresses each run. Patterns must be module-relative or use RIP-relative addressing.
Rules for how functions pass arguments and return values. Windows x64 uses RCX, RDX, R8, R9 then stack for args; caller cleans shadow space.
Memory scanner and debugger for Windows. Used for exact/unknown scans, pointer maps, and tracing writes to addresses in lab environments.
Lifts machine code to high-level pseudocode (e.g. Ghidra P-Code). Faster than raw disassembly for understanding control flow.
Dynamic Link Library — shared code loaded at runtime. Game engines and renderers are often separate DLLs with exports and imports.
Kernel structure representing a Windows process. User-mode tools see PID/handles; kernel debugging reveals full EPROCESS fields.
Dynamic memory region for allocations (malloc/new). Game entities and managers often live on the heap with changing addresses.
Unity pipeline that compiles C# to C++ then native code. Requires Il2CppDumper + Ghidra scripts for named functions.
I/O Control code — user-mode communicates with kernel drivers via DeviceIoControl. Common in anti-cheat driver architectures.
Load address of an EXE or DLL in a process. Offsets are often expressed as moduleBase + RVA for stable references.
Unity scripting backend shipping managed Assembly-CSharp.dll. Can be decompiled to C# with dnSpy or ILSpy.
User-mode gateway to the Windows kernel. Exports Nt/Zw APIs that transition via syscall instruction.
Windows executable format for .exe and .dll. Contains headers, sections, imports, exports, and relocations.
Process Environment Block — user-mode struct with image base, loader data, and BeingDebugged. Accessed via GS:[0x60] on x64.
Sequence of pointer dereferences with offsets from a static base to reach dynamic heap data across game restarts.
Run-Time Type Information — MSVC metadata linking vtables to type names. Helps identify C++ classes in binaries.
Relative Virtual Address — offset from module base to a symbol or instruction. Used to correlate Ghidra and debugger addresses.
CPU instruction transferring control from ring 3 to ring 0 with a service number in EAX on Windows x64.
Thread Environment Block — per-thread data including stack limits, TLS slots, and last error. GS points to TEB on x64.
Virtual function table — array of function pointers at the start of C++ polymorphic objects. Key to identifying class methods.
Isolated virtual machine for malware/RE work. Never analyze live multiplayer games or production anti-cheat on your main OS.
Link between code and data — who reads/writes an address or who calls a function. Core navigation in static analysis.
x64 addressing mode using instruction pointer + displacement. Common for globals; wildcards needed in AOB patterns for displacements.
Debug breakpoint using CPU DR0–DR3 registers. Breaks on execute/read/write without patching 0xCC into code.
